title: elymus trachycaulus subsp. novae-angliae
intro: Elymus trachycaulus subsp. novae-angliae is a subspecies of the Elymus trachycaulus grass, commonly known as slough grass or bearded wheat grass. It is a perennial grass native to the mountainous regions of North America. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties that help to soothe wounds, reduce fever, and relieve digestive issues. The herbal preparations of Elymus trachycaulus subsp. novae-angliae include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to promote overall health and well-being, including reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and boosting the immune system.
